test/app/custominvoicing.purpose: Integration tests for the custom invoicing app validating the async sync protocol (draft→issuing→payment) and invoice event JSON marshaling against a real Postgres DB via billingtest.BaseSuite. Primary constraint: all service interactions must go through BillingService and CustomInvoicingService — never adapter or Ent directly.

patterns.name: Embed billingtest.BaseSuite
patterns.description: All test suites embed billingtest.BaseSuite (not app/common wiring) to get BillingService, CustomerService, AppService, MockStreamingConnector wired from raw package constructors.

patterns.name: setupDefaultBillingProfile helper
patterns.description: Each test scenario calls s.setupDefaultBillingProfile(ctx, namespace, config) to install the custom invoicing app, configure it, and provision the billing profile before any invoice operations. DraftPeriod is set to P0D so lines immediately become invoiceable.

patterns.name: Drive state machine via service layer only
patterns.description: Invoice lifecycle is driven exclusively through s.BillingService and s.CustomInvoicingService (SyncDraftInvoice, SyncIssuingInvoice, HandlePaymentTrigger) — never through adapter or Ent directly.

patterns.name: Unique namespace per test function
patterns.description: Every test function uses a unique string literal namespace to avoid cross-test DB contamination without teardown.

patterns.name: MockStreamingConnector with deferred Reset
patterns.description: Usage data injected via s.MockStreamingConnector.AddSimpleEvent before invoicing; always defer s.MockStreamingConnector.Reset() to prevent stale events bleeding into subsequent tests.

patterns.name: Suite.Run for sub-scenarios
patterns.description: Related invoice lifecycle steps grouped with s.Run('description', func(){}) to share invoice state between steps and produce hierarchical test output.
patterns.example: s.Run("invoice can be created", func() { invoices, err := s.BillingService.InvoicePendingLines(...) })

key_file_guides.role: Main integration test covering full invoice flow (hooks enabled) and payment-status-only flow (hooks disabled); also provides setupDefaultBillingProfile helper used by event_test.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DraftPeriod is set to P0D in setupDefaultBillingProfile so lines immediately become invoiceable — changing this requires adjusting AsOf in InvoicePendingLines or assertions will fail. The two test functions cover two distinct state machine paths: hooks-enabled produces draft.syncing; hooks-disabled produces payment_processing.pending directly.

key_file_guides.role: Unit-level JSON round-trip test for billing.StandardInvoiceCreatedEvent — validates that app bases and appcustominvoicing.Meta survive marshal/unmarshal intact.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() rather than t.Context() — acceptable here since no cancellation semantics are tested; new tests should prefer t.Context().

anti_patterns: Calling adapter or Ent methods directly instead of going through BillingService / CustomInvoicingService
Importing app/common wiring into test setup — use raw package constructors like billingtest.BaseSuite
Sharing namespace strings across test functions — always use a unique namespace per top-level test
Forgetting defer s.MockStreamingConnector.Reset() — stale events bleed into subsequent tests
Setting DraftPeriod to a non-zero duration without adjusting AsOf — lines won't be invoiceable

decisions.decision: Tests embed billingtest.BaseSuite instead of constructing services manually
decisions.rationale: Keeps test setup DRY and independent from app/common to avoid import cycles; BaseSuite provides all shared services from underlying constructors.

decisions.decision: Both EnableDraftSyncHook=true and EnableDraftSyncHook=false flows are exercised in separate test functions
decisions.rationale: Custom invoicing has two distinct state machine paths: full async sync (draft→issuing sync hooks) and payment-status-only (skips sync hooks, goes directly to payment_processing.pending); both paths need explicit coverage.

code_examples.scenario: Full invoice lifecycle with sync hooks enabled

test/app/stripe.purpose: Integration and unit tests for the Stripe app covering app install/get/update/uninstall lifecycle, customer data CRUD, invoice sync to Stripe, and session creation — all using mock Stripe clients to avoid real API calls. Primary constraint: never import app/common; wire all services manually from raw package constructors.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface for app lifecycle tests
patterns.description: app_test.go and appstripe.go use a TestEnv interface (App, AppStripe, Billing, Customer, Fixture, Secret, StripeClient, StripeAppClient, Close) provisioned by NewTestEnv — services wired manually without app/common.

patterns.name: StripeClientMock / StripeAppClientMock with Restore()
patterns.description: Mock expectations are set per-test with .On(...).Return(...); always defer Restore() after each setup to clear ExpectedCalls[:0] without recreating the mock.

patterns.name: Embed billingtest.BaseSuite for invoice tests
patterns.description: StripeInvoiceTestSuite embeds billingtest.BaseSuite and builds extra services (SecretService, AppStripeService, Charges, LedgerResolver) in SetupSuite using raw constructors, not app/common wiring.
patterns.example: type StripeInvoiceTestSuite struct { billingtest.BaseSuite; AppStripeService appstripe.Service; Fixture *Fixture; StripeAppClient *StripeAppClientMock; Charges charges.Service }

patterns.name: Fixture for shared setup sequences
patterns.description: fixture.go provides setupApp, setupCustomer, setupAppWithCustomer to avoid duplicating Stripe mock setup and app installation across tests. setupApp calls Restore() via defer after each setup.
patterns.example: testApp, customer, customerData, err := s.Env.Fixture().setupAppWithCustomer(ctx, s.namespace)

patterns.name: Unique namespace per test via setupNamespace(t)
patterns.description: Each test in AppHandlerTestSuite calls s.setupNamespace(t) which sets s.namespace = ulid.Make().String() to guarantee isolation.

patterns.name: StableSortInvoiceItemParams for deterministic line matching
patterns.description: StripeAppClientMock.AddInvoiceLines calls StableSortInvoiceItemParams to sort line items by Description before mock matching, making assertions order-independent.
patterns.example: func (c *StripeAppClientMock) AddInvoiceLines(ctx context.Context, input stripeclient.AddInvoiceLinesInput) ... { c.StableSortInvoiceItemParams(input.Lines); args := c.Called(input) }

key_file_guides.role: Constructs the complete test dependency graph for app_test.go: Postgres via testutils.InitPostgresDB, customer/app/secret/stripe services wired manually, closerFunc for cleanup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New services added to the stripe test env must be added to both the testEnv struct and the TestEnv interface. Uses context.Background() in NewTestEnv — acceptable for env setup.

key_file_guides.role: Implements stripeclient.StripeClient and stripeclient.StripeAppClient with testify/mock; includes StableSortInvoiceItemParams/StableSortStripeInvoiceItemWithID to normalize line item order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Restore() clears c.ExpectedCalls[:0] — forgetting to call it leaks expectations across tests causing spurious failures in subsequent tests.

key_file_guides.role: Deep invoice sync tests with StripeInvoiceTestSuite embedding billingtest.BaseSuite; builds Stripe mock call sequences for CreateInvoice/AddInvoiceLines/FinalizeInvoice and verifies exact Stripe API call shapes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Stripe mock calls must match exact input structs including sorted line items — use expectStripeInvoiceAddLines helper which matches by Description key rather than index order. Ledger-backed tests (invoice_credits_test.go) require createStripeLedgerBackedCustomer which calls EnsureBusinessAccounts and CreateCustomerAccounts before any charge creation.

key_file_guides.role: Tests progressive billing with credit allocation, covering full credit-then-invoice settlement mode across partial and final invoices synced to Stripe.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses charges.NewChargeIntent (not struct literal Charge{}) and mustSettleExternalCreditPurchase to advance credit purchase through Authorized→Settled states before usage charges are created. clock.FreezeTime must be set appropriately for each invoice advancement step.

key_file_guides.role: Shared setup helpers for Stripe app install, customer creation, and customer data attachment. getStripeAccountId generates a random account ID each call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests calling setupApp twice in the same test will get different Stripe account IDs — configure mock to handle both or use a single setupAppWithCustomer call.

key_file_guides.role: MockSecretService wraps real secretservice with optional mock interception; mockEnabled=false (passthrough) by default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: var _ secret.SecretService = (*MockSecretService)(nil) compile check enforces interface completeness — add new methods to MockSecretService when secret.Service interface gains new methods.

anti_patterns: Calling Stripe client methods directly in tests instead of going through app.Service or appstripe.Service
Forgetting defer stripeClient.Restore() / stripeAppClient.Restore() — leftover mock expectations cause subsequent tests to fail
Importing app/common in test env construction — always wire from raw package constructors (appadapter.New, appservice.New, etc.)
Reusing the same namespace across multiple test functions in AppHandlerTestSuite — always call setupNamespace(t) at the start of each test method
Adding invoice line assertions without stable-sorting lines — use expectStripeInvoiceAddLines or StableSortInvoiceItemParams before setting mock expectations on AddInvoiceLines

decisions.decision: Two parallel test architectures: TestEnv interface (app_test.go) vs billingtest.BaseSuite embedding (invoice_test.go)
decisions.rationale: App lifecycle tests need a narrow env without the full billing stack; invoice tests need BaseSuite for BillingService, MockStreamingConnector, and meter setup — different scopes justified different setups.

decisions.decision: MockSecretService wraps real secretservice with Enable/Disable toggle rather than pure mock
decisions.rationale: Most tests need real secret persistence (storing Stripe API keys); only error-path tests need mock control, so passthrough-by-default avoids brittle expectation setup in the happy path.

decisions.decision: Stripe client mock sorts invoice line items before matching
decisions.rationale: Billing line generation order is non-deterministic; stable sorting in the mock (StableSortInvoiceItemParams) makes test assertions order-independent without requiring production code to sort.

code_examples.scenario: Install a Stripe app and verify it can be retrieved
